name: DefinitionList
summary: A DefinitionList object holds a list of all of the ComponentDefinition objects in a model. This class contains methods for adding and retrieving definitions from the list.

methods.summary: The [] method is used to retrieve a component definition from the list.
methods.remarks: The [] method is used to retrieve a component definition from the list. You can give an integer index in the range 0 to length, a string which represents the GUID (a unique internal identifier), or a string that is the name of the definition.

params.name: index
params.doc: The index for a specific component definition.

methods.name: add
methods.summary: The add method is used to add a new component definition to the definition list with the given name.
methods.remarks: The add method is used to add a new component definition to the definition list with the given name.

params.name: def_name
params.doc: The new component definition to add to the definition list.

returns.doc: the ComponentDefinition object that was added (if successful)

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.summary: The [] method is used to retrieve a component definition from the list.
methods.remarks: The [] method is used to retrieve a component definition from the list. You can give an integer index in the range 0 to length, a string which represents the GUID (a unique internal identifier), or a string that is the name of the definition.

params.name: index
params.doc: The index for a specific component definition.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elements to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method is used to iterate through all of the component definitions in the definition list.

methods.name: import
methods.summary: The #import method is used to import a (non-SketchUp) 3d model file as a definition.
methods.remarks: The #import method is used to import a (non-SketchUp) 3d model file as a definition. Importers using the C API SketchUpModelImporterInterface interface are supported (those in the Importers/ directory). See the Importer Options file for information on creating a valid hash for the various importers. For SketchUp models, instead use #load.

throws.when: if no suitable importer could be found.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is used to retrieve number of component definitions in the list.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to retrieve number of component definitions in the list.

methods.name: load
methods.signature: load(path) => Sketchup::ComponentDefinition | load(path, allow_newer: false) => Sketchup::ComponentDefinition
methods.summary: The #load method is used to load a component from a file.
methods.remarks: The #load method is used to load a component from a file.

params.name: path
params.doc: The path where the component definition file is located.

methods.name: load_from_url
methods.summary: The #load_from_url method loads a component from a location specified by string url.
methods.remarks: The #load_from_url method loads a component from a location specified by string url. This method throws an exception if an url string is not given, or an error occurs during retrieval from URL and a load_handler was not given.

params.name: url
params.doc: URL to load a .skp file from.

methods.name: purge_unused
methods.summary: The purge_unused method is used to remove the unused component definitions.
methods.remarks: The purge_unused method is used to remove the unused component definitions.

methods.name: remove
methods.summary: The #remove method is used to remove a component definition from the definition list with the given component definition.
methods.remarks: The #remove method is used to remove a component definition from the definition list with the given component definition. This will remove all instances of the definition.

throws.when: if the given definition is used by Model#active_path.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias for #length.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: unique_name
methods.summary: The unique_name is used to generate a unique name for a definition based on a base_name string.
methods.remarks: The unique_name is used to generate a unique name for a definition based on a base_name string. For example, a base_name of “Joe” might return “Joe #2”
